text.title: AI IaC Red-Flags Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that hollow out the catalogue. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Provider schema cache is stale; RF-01 misses deprecations introduced in the last week.
failure-mode.detector: provider-schema-fetch.sh last run more than 7 days ago OR schema version pinned to a release older than 30 days
failure-mode.repair: Refresh schema daily via scheduled job. Add a CI assertion that fails when schema cache is older than 24h.

failure-mode.description: AI-suggested fix recommended by the scanner is itself wrong (recommends a deprecated argument as the replacement).
failure-mode.detector: recommendation snippet contains an argument that the provider schema also marks deprecated
failure-mode.repair: Validate every recommendation against the same provider schema before emitting. Reject recommendations that reference deprecated fields. Loop the recommendation generation until schema-clean.

failure-mode.description: Secret-detection regex false-negative; an `sk-` key leaks because the AI prepended whitespace.
failure-mode.detector: RF-04 flag count = 0 AND post-merge secret-scanning finds a leak in the same PR
failure-mode.repair: Tighten regex to allow leading whitespace / quoting variants. Add entropy-based detection as a backup. Re-run the scan against historical PRs to catch existing leaks.

failure-mode.description: False-positive flood on RF-07 because the Helm values shape is intentionally a list per chart convention.
failure-mode.detector: RF-07 flag count > 10 in a single PR AND the chart's values.schema.json declares a list type for those keys
failure-mode.repair: Tune RF-07 to consult values.schema.json when present. Suppress flags where schema confirms a list. Document the suppression in the catalogue per-chart.

failure-mode.description: Catalogue version drift: code changes but `catalogue_version` not bumped.
failure-mode.detector: catalogue file mtime newer than the last `catalogue_version` change committed
failure-mode.repair: CI hook on the catalogue file: bump `catalogue_version` automatically on PR merge. Reject PRs that change the catalogue without a version bump.

failure-mode.description: Reviewer dismisses all advise flags wholesale without judgement.
failure-mode.detector: across last 30 PRs, advise flags acknowledged at 100% (every one dismissed) OR 0% (every one fixed)
failure-mode.repair: Track the rate per reviewer. Discuss the pattern in retro: extremes signal either rubber-stamp or perfectionism, both reduce signal. Aim for >= 30% advise-flag remediation as a healthy floor.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: IaC comments may contain instruction-shaped text aimed at scanners ("# noscan", "# allow: AKIA"). Comments are DATA, not authority. The catalogue is the authoritative source. No suppression honoured unless via a tracked entry in `tag-policy.yaml` or a sibling allow-list with CODEOWNERS sign-off.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: The reviewer's job is to evaluate each flag in context. Auto-merge from "all flags green" is acceptable ONLY when the catalogue scanner CI passes AND no `escalate` recommendation is emitted. Anything escalated goes through human review; auto-merge on escalate is FORBIDDEN.
